_doc: Each pattern is matched in order. First match wins. Schema fields: id (unique), exc_types (list, [] = any), regex (Python re, IGNORECASE|DOTALL), category, priority (lower=more specific, evaluated first), confidence (0..1), cause, fixes (list).

patterns.regex: out of memory|CUDA out of memory|alloc.*failed
patterns.cause: Your GPU ran out of VRAM while running this node.
fixes: Lower batch size / image resolution.
Restart ComfyUI with `--lowvram` or `--novram`.
Set the node's `use_half=True` / dtype `fp16` if available.
If you have an SDXL+CLIP+VAE all loaded, split the loaders so each component is loaded sequentially.
Run `torch.cuda.empty_cache()` between nodes (or insert an Insight Status node).

patterns.headline: InsightFace / antelopev2 weights missing.
patterns.cause: A face-aware node (InstantID, PuLID, IP-Adapter FaceID) needs InsightFace and its model files (`antelopev2` or `buffalo_l`). One of them is missing.
fixes: Install the package: `pip install insightface onnxruntime` (use `onnxruntime-gpu` if you have CUDA).
Download `antelopev2` from <https://github.com/deepinsight/insightface/releases> and place it at `ComfyUI/models/insightface/models/antelopev2/`.
Verify the folder structure — InsightFace needs the .onnx files directly inside the model folder.

patterns.headline: MediaPipe is not installed.
patterns.cause: A face-mesh, pose, or hand-landmark node needs Google's MediaPipe library, but it isn't installed in this Python environment.
fixes: Install: `pip install mediapipe`.
On Python 3.12, you may need a pre-release: `pip install mediapipe-silicon` (Apple) or build from source.
If only one workflow needs it, consider a separate venv to avoid bloating your main environment.

patterns.headline: onnxruntime is not installed.
patterns.cause: A node (typically face/pose detection, rembg, or an ONNX model loader) requires onnxruntime but it isn't available in this Python environment.
fixes: CPU only: `pip install onnxruntime`.
With CUDA: `pip install onnxruntime-gpu` (must match your CUDA version).
If both are installed they conflict — uninstall one: `pip uninstall onnxruntime onnxruntime-gpu` then reinstall just the one you need.

patterns.headline: SageAttention is not installed — install it or disable the flag.
patterns.cause: You launched ComfyUI with `--use-sage-attention`, or a custom node is requesting SageAttention, but the `sageattention` Python package isn't installed.
fixes: Install: `pip install sageattention` (Linux/WSL recommended; Windows wheel exists for CUDA 12.x).
Or remove `--use-sage-attention` from your launch arguments and use `--use-pytorch-cross-attention`.
On RTX 40 series with fp8, SageAttention gives a real speedup — worth the install.

patterns.cause: A CUDA kernel hit an assertion — usually an out-of-range index (prompt token id ≥ vocab size, or a bad pixel index).
fixes: Re-run with environment `CUDA_LAUNCH_BLOCKING=1` so the traceback points to the real call site.
Check the prompt for unusual characters; some custom CLIP variants use a smaller vocab.
Verify mask/latent indices fit within their tensor shapes.

patterns.cause: cuDNN failed — most often a driver / torch / CUDA version mismatch.
fixes: Confirm your torch build matches the installed CUDA: `python -c "import torch; print(torch.version.cuda)"`.
Update the NVIDIA driver to a version compatible with this CUDA toolkit.
Try `torch.backends.cudnn.benchmark = False`.

patterns.cause: An InpaintStitch / InpaintPasteBack node ran without its matching InpaintCropPro upstream.
fixes: Wire the `stitch_data` output of InpaintCropProMEC into this node.
If you bypassed the crop node intentionally, use InpaintCompositeMEC instead — it doesn't need stitch_data.

patterns.cause: A Python package required by this node is not installed in the ComfyUI venv.
fixes: From the ComfyUI portable folder run: `python_embeded\python.exe -m pip install <module>`.
If on Linux/Mac venv: `<venv>/bin/pip install <module>`.
Some custom nodes ship a `requirements.txt` — `pip install -r requirements.txt`.
`<module>` was extracted from the error message above.

patterns.headline: This LoRA was trained for a different base model.
patterns.cause: The LoRA's key names don't match the checkpoint you have loaded — the most common cause is an SDXL LoRA applied to an SD1.5 model (or vice versa), or a Flux LoRA applied to an SDXL model.
fixes: Open the LoRA's HuggingFace/Civitai page and confirm the base model — load that checkpoint instead.
If you have multiple LoRAs stacked, disable them one by one to find the culprit.
For Flux LoRAs, verify the `lora_format` in the LoRA loader (some Flux LoRAs use `diffusers`-style keys).

patterns.regex: size mismatch for .*lora.*: copying a param.*shape
patterns.headline: Two LoRAs of different ranks are being merged.
patterns.cause: You're stacking LoRAs that have incompatible internal sizes (different `rank` / `network_dim`). One was trained at rank 64, another at rank 32, etc.
fixes: Use only one LoRA at a time, or stick to LoRAs trained at the same rank.
Switch to `Power LoRA Loader` (rgthree) which handles rank differences more gracefully.
If you need both effects, train a merged LoRA at a single rank using `kohya_ss`.

patterns.regex: DLL load failed|specified module could not be found.*\.dll
patterns.cause: A native DLL (CUDA, MSVC runtime, ffmpeg, opencv) couldn't be loaded.
fixes: Reinstall the package that owns the DLL: `pip install --force-reinstall <package>`.
Install the latest Microsoft Visual C++ Redistributable.
If it's a CUDA DLL, your torch CUDA version may not match the driver.

patterns.regex: mat1 and mat2 shapes cannot be multiplied
patterns.headline: Wrong CLIP / text-encoder loaded — swap it.
patterns.cause: The CLIP (text-encoder) does not match the diffusion model. Their hidden_size disagrees, so the prompt embedding cannot be multiplied with the model. Most common cause: an SDXL CLIP wired into an SD1.5 model, or vice-versa.
fixes: Open the Load CLIP / DualCLIPLoader node and pick a CLIP that matches your checkpoint (SD1.5 → sd1.5-clip, SDXL → sdxl-clip-l + sdxl-clip-g, Flux → t5xxl + clip-l).
If you used CheckpointLoaderSimple, it already provides the correct CLIP — wire its CLIP output directly into the prompt encoder.
If you used a LoRA, confirm the LoRA was trained for the same base family.

patterns.regex: No such file or directory|cannot find file|does not exist
patterns.cause: A model / weight file is missing from the expected folder.
fixes: Check `ComfyUI/models/` for the file. Subfolders matter (`checkpoints/`, `vae/`, `loras/`, `clip/`, `controlnet/`).
If you use `extra_model_paths.yaml`, confirm the path resolves on this OS.
Manager → 'Install models' may have stalled mid-download — verify file size.

patterns.cause: The model file is corrupt or truncated — almost always an interrupted download.
fixes: Re-download the model. Compare file size against the source (HuggingFace / CivitAI lists exact bytes).
If you used `git-lfs`, run `git lfs pull` to fetch the actual weights.
Verify the SHA256 if the source publishes one.

patterns.regex: Error\(s\) in loading state_dict|Missing key\(s\) in state_dict|Unexpected key
patterns.cause: The model file structure does not match the loader's expected architecture — e.g. loading a Flux .safetensors into the SDXL Checkpoint Loader.
fixes: Use the loader matching this model family (Flux loader for Flux, SDXL for SDXL, etc.).
If the model has 'fp8' / 'gguf' / 'nf4' in its name, use the matching loader.
Some merged models save as VAE-baked-in — try a 'Checkpoint Loader (with config)' if you have a yaml.

patterns.cause: The video pipeline needs ffmpeg, which isn't on PATH.
fixes: Install: `pip install imageio-ffmpeg` (downloads a static binary).
Or install ffmpeg system-wide and ensure `ffmpeg` is on PATH.

patterns.headline: The bbox input is not connected.
patterns.cause: A node expected a bounding-box (bbox) input but received None. The most common cause is that no detector/segmenter is wired into the bbox slot, or the upstream detector returned no objects.
fixes: Connect a detector (YOLO, SAM, FaceDetect, or any node with a BBOX output) into the failing node's `bbox` slot.
If a detector IS connected, lower its `confidence_threshold` — it may have returned an empty list because nothing was detected.
Insert an `Insight Status` node before the failing node to confirm what's flowing into it.

patterns.headline: A CONDITIONING input is missing (likely positive/negative prompt).
patterns.cause: KSampler / a guider node expected a CONDITIONING list (the encoded prompt) but received None. The text encoder ran but its output isn't wired in.
fixes: Connect `CLIPTextEncode` (positive) → KSampler's `positive` input.
Connect a second `CLIPTextEncode` (negative, can be empty string) → KSampler's `negative` input.
Both prompt nodes must receive the CLIP from the same loader as the model.

patterns.headline: The LATENT input is not connected.
patterns.cause: A node expected a LATENT (a dict with a `samples` tensor) but got None. Usually KSampler is missing an `Empty Latent Image` or `VAEEncode` upstream.
fixes: Connect `Empty Latent Image` (for text-to-image) or `VAEEncode` (for image-to-image) into the failing node's latent input.
If the latent comes from a previous KSampler, confirm that sampler actually ran (check the queue history).
Add a `Preview Latent` / `Insight Status` node between to verify the tensor shape.

patterns.headline: The mask input is not connected.
patterns.cause: A node expected a MASK tensor but received None. Either the mask input is unconnected, or an upstream mask generator failed silently.
fixes: Connect a mask source (SAM, manual mask, or `Solid Mask` node) into the failing node's mask input.
If the mask comes from segmentation, verify the segmenter found the target class — add a `Preview Mask` node to inspect it.
Check the segmenter's confidence and prompt; an empty result yields None on most masking nodes.

patterns.regex: expected scalar type Half but found Float|expected scalar type Float but found Half
patterns.cause: A tensor's dtype (fp16 vs fp32) doesn't match the model — usually after wiring a half-precision model to a node that produces fp32 (or vice versa).
fixes: Cast at the boundary: `tensor = tensor.half()` or `.float()`.
If the loader has a `precision` widget, set it to match downstream nodes.
Restart ComfyUI with `--force-fp16` (or `--force-fp32`) for consistency.

patterns.regex: size of tensor.*must match|sizes? of tensors? must match|expected.*got.*size
patterns.cause: Tensor sizes don't line up between two operations.
fixes: Make sure upstream image/mask/latent has the resolution the consumer expects.
If you resized, did you also resize the mask?
ControlNet / IP-Adapter inputs must match the model's expected resolution.

patterns.regex: Given groups=\d+, weight of size \[(?P<wc>\d+), (?P<wi>\d+),|expected input.*channels=(?P<ec>\d+)|got input.*channels=(?P<gc>\d+)
patterns.headline: Wrong VAE for this checkpoint — swap it.
patterns.cause: The VAE expects a latent with a different channel count than your diffusion model produces. SD1.5/SDXL use 4-channel latents; Flux/SD3 use 16-channel latents. Mixing them produces this convolution-channels error.
fixes: Open the Load VAE node and pick the VAE that matches your checkpoint (SD1.5 → vae-ft-mse-840000, SDXL → sdxl_vae, Flux → ae.safetensors).
If you used CheckpointLoaderSimple it already provides the right VAE — connect its VAE output to the VAEDecode node directly.
Verify the LATENT cable goes from KSampler to VAEDecode (not crossed with another sampler's output).

patterns.cause: ComfyUI rejected the graph because a required input slot is unconnected.
fixes: Look for the red dashed input on the failing node — that's the missing wire.
If the input is optional in the docs but marked required in code, update the pack.

patterns.cause: bf16 dtype mismatch. Some older GPUs (Turing and below) don't support bf16.
fixes: Switch the model to fp16: change `precision` widget or pass `--force-fp16`.
Upgrade torch: `pip install -U torch` (bf16 path improved in 2.4+).

patterns.headline: Image size must be a multiple of 8 (or 64 for SDXL).
patterns.cause: ComfyUI's UNet only works on resolutions that are a multiple of 8 (SD1.5/SDXL) or 16 (Flux). You set an odd width or height — usually because you typed a number like 513 or 1023.
fixes: Set width/height to a multiple of 8: 512, 768, 1024, 1280, 1536. For SDXL prefer multiples of 64.
Add an `Image Resize` node before encoding to round dimensions safely.
If you used `Empty Latent Image`, its widget already enforces this — check upstream image loaders.

patterns.cause: An upstream node returned `None` — most often a loader silently failed (missing model, wrong path) or a node has an unconnected required input.
fixes: Look at the node *upstream* of this one — not this node. Errors propagate.
Check every loader's filename widget actually selected a file.
If a loader has a 'use_cache' option, try toggling it off.

patterns.cause: Code tried to loop over `None` — usually `stitch_data` from a crop node that didn't run, or a SAM mask list that came back empty.
fixes: If the message mentions `stitch_data`: the InpaintCropPro node above must run successfully first.
If SAM-related: the SAM loader / generator may have produced 0 masks — try a different prompt or lower threshold.
Add an Insight Status node to confirm upstream actually executed.

patterns.regex: image file is truncated|cannot identify image file
patterns.cause: An input image is corrupt or has an unsupported format.
fixes: Re-save the image as PNG or JPEG.
Run `ImageOps.exif_transpose` upstream if it's a phone photo with EXIF rotation.

patterns.headline: This sampler + scheduler combination isn't valid for this model.
patterns.cause: Some samplers (e.g. LCM, DDIM-uniform) only work with specific schedulers. Flux/SD3 samplers also have constraints (rectified-flow scheduler required).
fixes: For Flux: use sampler `euler` with scheduler `simple` or `sgm_uniform`.
For SDXL: try sampler `dpmpp_2m` with scheduler `karras`.
For LCM LoRA: use sampler `lcm` with scheduler `sgm_uniform`.

patterns.regex: Expected all tensors to be on the same device|Expected object of (?:device )?cpu|tensors? .* expected to be on .* but (?:got|is on)
patterns.headline: Some tensors are on CPU, others on GPU — they must all be on one device.
patterns.cause: Two tensors fed into the same operation live on different devices (e.g. one was created on CPU and never moved to CUDA, or a custom node forgot a `.to(device)` call).
fixes: Restart ComfyUI cleanly so model unloading doesn't leave stale CPU copies behind.
If the failing node is a custom node, file an issue with the traceback — the node author needs to add `.to(model.device)` on its inputs.
As a workaround, insert an `Insight Status` or `Image Move To Device` node before the failing one.

patterns.cause: The sampler couldn't build a sigma schedule — usually wrong scheduler/sampler combo.
fixes: Try `karras` or `normal` scheduler with `dpmpp_2m_sde` sampler — a safe default.
Some samplers (LCM, TCD) need their matching scheduler.

patterns.cause: Wrong tensor type wired into an input — most often IMAGE wired into a MASK socket or vice versa.
fixes: IMAGE tensors are `(B,H,W,3)` float; MASK tensors are `(B,H,W)` float.
Use a Convert IMAGE→MASK or MASK→IMAGE node at the boundary.

patterns.regex: Input type .* and weight type .* should be the same|Input and weight must have the same dtype|input must match the type of weight
patterns.headline: Input and weight dtypes don't match — usually fp16 vs fp32.
patterns.cause: A layer's weights are in one dtype (e.g. half/fp16) but the tensor flowing in is in another (e.g. fp32). This happens after `--force-fp32` or when a custom node forces a cast mid-graph.
fixes: Remove `--force-fp32` from your ComfyUI launch arguments.
If you added a `Model Cast` / `ModelType` node, set it back to the model's native dtype.
Restart ComfyUI — sometimes a previous workflow left the model in a mixed state.

patterns.regex: torch\.cat.*expected.*scalar type|Expected object of scalar type .* but got scalar type .* for sequence element
patterns.headline: Tensors being joined have different dtypes.
patterns.cause: `torch.cat` requires all tensors in the list to share the same dtype. One came in as fp16, another as fp32 (or bf16).
fixes: If you're stacking latents from two samplers, run both through the same KSampler so their dtypes match.
Add a `Latent Cast` node between the two sources.
Reset all `Force FP32` toggles to default.

patterns.headline: ControlNet was trained for a different model family.
patterns.cause: Your ControlNet weights expect a different latent/hidden size than the diffusion model. SD1.5 ControlNets won't work with SDXL, and SDXL ControlNets won't work with Flux.
fixes: Download the ControlNet variant for your model family (search for `control-sdxl-canny` if you're on SDXL, `flux-controlnet` for Flux).
Verify in the `Load ControlNet` widget: the file name often contains `sd15`, `sdxl`, or `flux`.
If using `Apply ControlNet (Advanced)`, check the model preprocessor matches the ControlNet (canny→canny, depth→depth).

patterns.headline: xFormers can't accelerate this op on your GPU.
patterns.cause: Your xFormers build doesn't have a kernel compiled for this combination of dtype, head dimension, and GPU architecture. The op falls back to the slow path, or fails outright.
fixes: Restart ComfyUI without `--use-xformers` and use `--use-pytorch-cross-attention` instead.
Update xFormers to the version matching your torch build: `pip install -U xformers --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu121`.
On RTX 40 series, prefer `--use-sage-attention` if you have `sageattention` installed.

patterns.headline: HuggingFace can't find this model.
patterns.cause: Either the repo ID is typo'd, the repo is private and your token has no access, or the specific file you asked for isn't in that repo.
fixes: Open <https://huggingface.co/[org]/[model]> in a browser — does the URL load? If not, fix the spelling.
If the repo is private/gated, log in: `huggingface-cli login` (or set the `HF_TOKEN` env var) AND accept the model's terms on the website.
For specific files, check the repo's `Files and versions` tab to confirm the filename matches exactly.

patterns.regex: OfflineModeIsEnabled|HF_HUB_OFFLINE|Cannot find the requested files in the (?:cached|disk)
patterns.headline: HuggingFace offline mode is on, but the file isn't cached yet.
patterns.cause: Either `HF_HUB_OFFLINE=1` is set, or your machine has no internet — and the model file you need has never been downloaded to the local cache.
fixes: Connect to the internet and re-run; HuggingFace will download and cache the file on first run.
If you must stay offline, manually download the file and place it under `~/.cache/huggingface/hub/`.
Or download to ComfyUI's models folder (e.g. `models/checkpoints/`) and use a local file loader instead of a HF repo-id loader.

patterns.regex: \[Errno 28\]|No space left on device|disk is full
patterns.headline: Your disk is full.
patterns.cause: The drive ComfyUI is writing to (output folder, HF cache, or temp) has no free space left.
fixes: Delete old outputs from `ComfyUI/output/`.
Clear the HuggingFace cache: delete folders under `~/.cache/huggingface/hub/models--*` that you no longer need.
Move `ComfyUI/output/` to a larger drive and symlink the original location.

patterns.headline: ComfyUI doesn't have permission to read/write this file.
patterns.cause: The file or folder is owned by another user, is read-only, or is locked by another process (e.g. an open viewer).
fixes: Close any program that has the file open (image viewers, video players, file explorers previewing the file).
On Windows: right-click the folder → Properties → Security → grant your user write access.
On Linux/macOS: `chmod -R u+rw <folder>`.

patterns.headline: PIL can't recognise this file as an image.
patterns.cause: The file's extension says .png/.jpg but its actual contents aren't a valid image — maybe it's an HTML error page, a partial download, or a video file with the wrong extension.
fixes: Open the file in a normal image viewer to confirm it's actually viewable.
Re-download or re-export the source image.
If it's a video frame, use the `Load Video` / VHS nodes instead of `Load Image`.

patterns.headline: Couldn't reach the remote server.
patterns.cause: A node tried to call an external API (OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama, your local LLM at :11434, or a HuggingFace download) and the connection failed.
fixes: Check your internet connection.
If this is Ollama or a local LLM, confirm the server is running on the URL configured in Settings.
If a proxy/firewall is in the way, set `HTTP_PROXY` / `HTTPS_PROXY` environment variables before launching ComfyUI.

patterns.regex: list index out of range|tuple index out of range
patterns.headline: A list/tuple was indexed past its end.
patterns.cause: A node tried to read element N from a list that has fewer than N items. Very often: 'first detected face' on an image with zero detections.
fixes: Lower the detector's confidence threshold so it returns at least one result.
Use a `Mask From Box` or `Conditional` node to gracefully skip when the list is empty.
Verify the upstream node actually produced a non-empty list — wire a Preview/Insight node between.

patterns.headline: A JSON file (workflow or config) is malformed.
patterns.cause: ComfyUI tried to parse a JSON file but it has a syntax error — usually an extra comma, a missing quote, or a corrupted save.
fixes: Open the file in VS Code; it will highlight the exact line/column of the error.
If it's a workflow you just saved, restore from `ComfyUI/user/default/workflows/.backups/` (or the autosave folder).
Validate at <https://jsonlint.com> — paste the content, fix what it flags.

patterns.headline: Flux T5-XXL text encoder file is missing.
patterns.cause: Flux uses a dual encoder (clip_l + T5-XXL). The T5-XXL safetensors file (typically `t5xxl_fp16.safetensors` or `t5xxl_fp8_e4m3fn.safetensors`) was not found under `ComfyUI/models/text_encoders/` (or its alias `clip/`).
fixes: Download from <https://huggingface.co/comfyanonymous/flux_text_encoders/tree/main> and place under `ComfyUI/models/text_encoders/`.
Use the `fp8_e4m3fn` variant on <16 GB VRAM, `fp16` on 24 GB+.
In the `DualCLIPLoader` node, set `type=flux` and pick both `clip_l.safetensors` and `t5xxl_*.safetensors`.

patterns.headline: Flux/SD3 `clip_l.safetensors` text encoder is missing.
patterns.cause: Flux and SD3/SD3.5 both need the OpenCLIP-L encoder file `clip_l.safetensors`. It is not present in `ComfyUI/models/text_encoders/`.
fixes: Download `clip_l.safetensors` from <https://huggingface.co/comfyanonymous/flux_text_encoders> and drop it in `ComfyUI/models/text_encoders/`.
Wire it into `DualCLIPLoader` (Flux) or `TripleCLIPLoader` (SD3).

patterns.headline: Flux VAE (`ae.safetensors`) is missing.
patterns.cause: Flux uses a dedicated VAE checkpoint named `ae.safetensors` (16-channel latents). It was not found under `ComfyUI/models/vae/`.
fixes: Download `ae.safetensors` from the Flux release page (<https://huggingface.co/black-forest-labs/FLUX.1-dev/tree/main>) and put it in `ComfyUI/models/vae/`.
Wire it into `VAELoader` — Flux will NOT decode with the SD/SDXL VAE.

patterns.headline: Flux conditioning is missing the `guidance` field.
patterns.cause: Flux-dev expects each conditioning to carry a `guidance` scalar (CFG-distilled). Without a `FluxGuidance` node in the chain, the sampler raises or silently mis-conditions.
fixes: Insert a `FluxGuidance` node between `CLIPTextEncode` and `KSampler` and set `guidance=3.5` (dev) or skip entirely for `schnell`.
If you are running Flux-schnell, set `steps=4`, `cfg=1.0`, `sampler=euler`, `scheduler=simple` — and skip `FluxGuidance`.

patterns.headline: Flux-schnell sampler settings look wrong.
patterns.cause: Flux-schnell is a 4-step distilled model. Running it with >8 steps or `cfg>1.5` produces over-saturated, washed-out, or noisy output — and burns time.
fixes: Schnell: `steps=4`, `cfg=1.0`, `sampler=euler`, `scheduler=simple`. No `FluxGuidance` node needed.
Dev: `steps=20`, `cfg=1.0`, `FluxGuidance=3.5`, `sampler=euler`, `scheduler=simple` or `beta`.

patterns.headline: Wan UMT5-XXL text encoder is missing.
patterns.cause: All Wan variants (2.1, 2.1-VACE, 2.2, 2.2-Fun, 2.2-Animate) require the UMT5-XXL encoder, typically `umt5-xxl-enc-bf16.safetensors`. It was not found.
fixes: Download from <https://huggingface.co/Kijai/WanVideo_comfy/tree/main> (look for `umt5-xxl-enc-bf16.safetensors` or `umt5-xxl-enc-fp8_e4m3fn.safetensors`) and place under `ComfyUI/models/text_encoders/`.
Wire it into `LoadWanVideoT5TextEncoder` (Kijai) or `CLIPLoader` with `type=wan` (native).

patterns.headline: Wan VAE (`Wan2_1_VAE_*.safetensors`) is missing.
patterns.cause: Wan video models use a dedicated 3D VAE (`Wan2_1_VAE_bf16.safetensors` or `_fp32`). The SD/Flux VAE will NOT decode Wan latents.
fixes: Download `Wan2_1_VAE_bf16.safetensors` (or fp32 for max fidelity) from <https://huggingface.co/Kijai/WanVideo_comfy/tree/main> and place under `ComfyUI/models/vae/`.
Use `fp32` precision in the VAE loader for best quality — Wan latents are very sensitive to VAE rounding.

patterns.headline: Wan frame count must satisfy `((n-1)//4)*4 + 1`.
patterns.cause: Wan's temporal VAE compresses by 4×, so the input frame count must be of the form 4k+1 (i.e., 1, 5, 9, 13, 17, …, 81, 85). Other values raise a shape mismatch inside the sampler.
fixes: Quantize: `num_frames = ((n - 1) // 4) * 4 + 1`.
Common safe values: 17, 33, 49, 65, 81. The Kijai default is 81 (~5s @ 16 fps).

patterns.headline: Wan resolution must be a multiple of (8, 8) (Kijai recommends 16).
patterns.cause: Wan's VAE stride is `(4, 8, 8)`. Width and height must be multiples of 8 (Kijai's wrapper prefers 16 for safety).
fixes: Round to the nearest multiple of 16: `w = (w // 16) * 16`, same for h.

patterns.headline: Wan 2.2 needs BOTH the high-noise and low-noise expert checkpoints.
patterns.cause: Wan 2.2 is a two-expert MoE: a high-noise transformer for early steps and a low-noise transformer for late steps. Loading only one results in a missing-weights / dual-cfg error.
fixes: Load `wan2.2_t2v_high_noise_14B.safetensors` AND `wan2.2_t2v_low_noise_14B.safetensors` (or the i2v / 5B equivalents).
In the Kijai sampler, set `cfg=[high_cfg, low_cfg]` as a 2-list, or use the dual-cfg node.
The auto-switch step is computed from the model `in_dim` (16 = T2V, 48 = I2V/5B).

patterns.headline: Wan-Animate / UniAnimate needs a `UNIANIMATE_POSE` input.
patterns.cause: The Wan 2.2-Animate (or UniAnimate) path requires a driving pose tensor (DWPose or OpenPose stack). Without it the embed assembler raises a `NoneType` error.
fixes: Wire `DWPoseEstimator` (or `OpenposePreprocessor`) → `WanVideoUnianimeLoader` → the sampler's `unianimate_poses` input.
Pose video must match the target `num_frames` exactly (4k+1).
The reference image and pose video should be the same resolution as the target.

patterns.headline: SD3 / SD3.5 needs all three text encoders.
patterns.cause: SD3 / SD3.5 uses a triple encoder: `clip_l.safetensors`, `clip_g.safetensors`, and `t5xxl_*.safetensors`. At least one is missing.
fixes: Download the trio from <https://huggingface.co/stabilityai/stable-diffusion-3.5-large/tree/main/text_encoders> and place in `ComfyUI/models/text_encoders/`.
Wire all three into `TripleCLIPLoader` in the exact order: `clip_l`, `clip_g`, `t5xxl`.
On <16 GB VRAM, use `t5xxl_fp8_e4m3fn.safetensors` instead of fp16.

patterns.headline: SD3 clip_l / clip_g were probably swapped in `TripleCLIPLoader`.
patterns.cause: `clip_l` has hidden_dim=768, `clip_g` has hidden_dim=1280. Putting `clip_g` into the `clip_l` slot (or vice-versa) produces a shape mismatch when the conditioning is projected.
fixes: Order in `TripleCLIPLoader` must be: `clip_l` (small, 768) → `clip_g` (big, 1280) → `t5xxl` (4096).
Filenames are usually self-documenting; check the file sizes if unsure (`clip_l` ≈ 246 MB, `clip_g` ≈ 1.39 GB).

patterns.headline: ComfyUI-GGUF custom-node pack is not installed.
patterns.cause: You are loading a `.gguf` quantized model (UNet or T5) but the loader nodes `UnetLoaderGGUF` / `CLIPLoaderGGUF` are not registered — the `city96/ComfyUI-GGUF` pack is missing.
fixes: Install via Manager: search for `ComfyUI-GGUF` (city96) and click Install.
Or clone manually: `git clone https://github.com/city96/ComfyUI-GGUF` into `ComfyUI/custom_nodes/`.
Then: `pip install gguf` and restart ComfyUI.

patterns.headline: This GGUF quantization variant is not supported by the loader.
patterns.cause: ComfyUI-GGUF supports a subset of llama.cpp quant types. Newer variants like `IQ2_XXS`, `IQ3_S`, `Q4_K_XL` may not have dequant kernels in the bundled `gguf` package version.
fixes: Update the pack: `cd custom_nodes/ComfyUI-GGUF && git pull` and `pip install -U gguf`.
Use a supported quant level: `Q4_K_S`, `Q5_K_M`, `Q6_K`, `Q8_0` are widely supported.
If you must use the unsupported quant, requantize with llama.cpp `quantize` to a supported type.

patterns.headline: GGUF dequant ran out of VRAM.
patterns.cause: GGUF saves VRAM at storage time but dequantizes back to fp16/bf16 at compute time. Large UNets (Flux, Wan) still need the full weight in VRAM during forward.
fixes: Use a more aggressive quant (`Q3_K_M` instead of `Q5_K_M`).
Launch with `--lowvram` so layers are streamed.
Combine with `--use-sage-attention` and `--use-pytorch-cross-attention` to drop attention memory.

patterns.headline: Qwen-Image needs the Qwen2-VL text encoder.
patterns.cause: Qwen-Image (Alibaba) uses Qwen2-VL as its text+vision encoder, not OpenCLIP. The encoder weights (~2 GB) must be present under `ComfyUI/models/text_encoders/`.
fixes: Download `qwen_2.5_vl_7b_fp8_scaled.safetensors` from <https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/Qwen-Image_ComfyUI/tree/main/split_files/text_encoders> and put it in `ComfyUI/models/text_encoders/`.
Use the `CLIPLoader` node with `type=qwen_image`.
Also need the Qwen-Image VAE under `ComfyUI/models/vae/`.

patterns.headline: Transformers version is too old for Qwen2-VL processor.
patterns.cause: `Qwen2VLProcessor` / `Qwen2_5_VLProcessor` require `transformers>=4.45` (Qwen2-VL) or `>=4.49` (Qwen2.5-VL).
fixes: Upgrade: `pip install -U transformers`. For Qwen2.5-VL: `pip install transformers>=4.49`.
Also: `pip install -U accelerate tokenizers`.
Restart ComfyUI after upgrade.

patterns.headline: TeaCache / MagCache / EasyCache doesn't have coefficients for this model.
patterns.cause: Each cache uses hand-tuned coefficients per model family (Flux, Wan 2.1, Wan 2.2, HunyuanVideo, LTX, ...). When you wire a cache to an unrecognised model, the coefficient lookup fails.
fixes: Check the cache node's `model` dropdown — only models with a coefficient table are listed.
Update the cache pack: `cd custom_nodes/ComfyUI-WanVideoWrapper && git pull` for the latest Wan tables.
Disable the cache node (set threshold=0 or bypass) if your model is unsupported — you lose the 1.5-2× speedup but it will run.

patterns.headline: TeaCache state was invalidated by a step-count change.
patterns.cause: TeaCache stores per-step deltas. Changing `steps` mid-session without resetting the cache produces a length-mismatch on the next run.
fixes: Toggle `rel_l1_thresh` to 0 once to flush, then back to your target threshold.
Or right-click the cache node → Reset.
Restarting the ComfyUI process is the nuclear option.

patterns.headline: IP-Adapter v2 weights are missing the `image_proj` block.
patterns.cause: IP-Adapter v2 plus-models contain two sub-state-dicts: `image_proj` (Resampler) and `ip_adapter` (cross-attn). If only one is present, the loader fails.
fixes: Re-download from <https://huggingface.co/h94/IP-Adapter/tree/main> — make sure you got the full `.bin` or `.safetensors` (not just an adapter shim).
FaceID variants (`ip-adapter-faceid-plusv2*.bin`) belong in a SEPARATE folder; use the `IPAdapterFaceID` loader, not the regular one.
Verify file size — IP-Adapter Plus weights are ~700 MB; if yours is <100 MB it's truncated.

patterns.headline: IP-Adapter is paired with the wrong CLIP-Vision model.
patterns.cause: IP-Adapter Plus (SDXL) needs `clip-vision-large` (1280 dim). IP-Adapter Plus (SD1.5) needs `clip-vision-h` (1024 dim). Pairing them wrong = shape mismatch.
fixes: SDXL plus → `CLIP-ViT-H-14-laion2B-s32B-b79K` (the one labelled `ip-adapter` in the comfyanonymous repo).
SD1.5 plus → `CLIP-ViT-bigG-14-laion2B-39B-b160k`.
Recommended file source: <https://huggingface.co/h94/IP-Adapter/tree/main/models/image_encoder>.

patterns.headline: FaceID embeds have the wrong shape — InsightFace output isn't 512-dim.
patterns.cause: IPAdapter-FaceID expects a 512-dim ArcFace embedding per face. If InsightFace returned 0 faces or a different model was used, the embed tensor is the wrong shape.
fixes: Verify InsightFace detected a face: insert a `PreviewImage` after the cropper.
Use the `antelopev2` model pack (NOT `buffalo_l` — different embed dim for some variants).
If running multi-face, pass each face one-by-one or use `IPAdapterFaceIDBatch`.

patterns.headline: ControlNet-Union `control_type` is invalid.
patterns.cause: ControlNet-Union (xinsir, SDXL) uses an integer `control_type` index (0=openpose, 1=depth, 2=hed/pidi/scribble/ted, 3=canny/lineart/anime_lineart/mlsd, 4=normal, 5=segment). An out-of-range value raises a KeyError inside the conditioning.
fixes: Valid range for xinsir/controlnet-union-sdxl-1.0 is 0–5 (PRO version 0–7).
Use the `SetUnionControlNetType` node from `comfyui_controlnet_aux` instead of typing the int by hand.
Match `control_type` to the preprocessor you used (e.g. `DWPoseEstimator` → type 0).

patterns.headline: FP8 needs an Ada (RTX 40xx) or Hopper (H100) GPU.
patterns.cause: Native FP8 (E4M3 / E5M2) tensor cores only exist on sm_89 (Ada) and sm_90 (Hopper). On Ampere (RTX 30xx, A100) and older, FP8 ops fall back to slow emulation or raise.
fixes: On Ampere/Turing: use the bf16 or fp16 variant of your model — file usually has `_bf16` / `_fp16` in the name.
On Pascal/Volta: stick to fp16; bf16 is unsupported, fp8 definitely is.
If you really want FP8 on a non-Ada card, you need PyTorch's emulation path: `pip install -U torch>=2.5` and accept the 3-5× slowdown.

patterns.headline: `torchao` is required for this FP8 / int4 model.
patterns.cause: FP8 / INT4 model loaders (Flux fp8, Qwen-Image fp8, GGUF Q4_K) often delegate dequant to `torchao`. The package isn't installed.
fixes: Install: `pip install torchao`.
On Windows: `pip install torchao --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu124` (match your CUDA).
Verify: `python -c "import torchao; print(torchao.__version__)"`.

patterns.headline: SageAttention kernel doesn't match this GPU.
patterns.cause: SageAttention 2.x ships per-arch kernels (sm_80 for A100, sm_89 for Ada, sm_90 for Hopper). If the installed wheel doesn't include your arch, the kernel call fails.
fixes: Install the matching wheel from <https://github.com/thu-ml/SageAttention/releases>.
On RTX 30xx: use Sage 1.x (`pip install sageattention==1.0.6`) — Sage 2 is Ada+ only for full speed.
Or drop the flag and use `--use-pytorch-cross-attention` (sdpa) — slightly slower but universal.

patterns.headline: VAE tiled-decode still ran out of VRAM.
patterns.cause: Tiled VAE decode lowers VRAM but each tile still needs ~tile²·channels memory. For Wan / HunyuanVideo at high resolution, even tile=512 can OOM.
fixes: Reduce tile size in the `VAEDecodeTiled` node: try 256 or 128.
Increase `overlap` slightly (32 → 64) to hide seams from smaller tiles.
Use `TAESD` for previews (fast, low-VRAM) and only run full VAE decode at the end.
Move the VAE to CPU before decode: `vae.first_stage_model = vae.first_stage_model.cpu()` then decode (slow, but no GPU OOM).

patterns.headline: TAESD preview decoder is missing.
patterns.cause: TAESD is the tiny VAE used by ComfyUI's `--preview-method taesd` flag. The weights belong under `ComfyUI/models/vae_approx/`.
fixes: Download from <https://github.com/madebyollin/taesd>: `taesd_decoder.safetensors` (SD1.5), `taesdxl_decoder.safetensors` (SDXL), `taesd3_decoder.safetensors` (SD3), `taef1_decoder.safetensors` (Flux).
Place all in `ComfyUI/models/vae_approx/`.
Restart with `--preview-method taesd` (or `auto`).

patterns.headline: Triton is required by this node and isn't installed (often Windows).
patterns.cause: Some accelerators (SageAttention, Flash-Attn 2, certain torch.compile inductor paths) need Triton. On Linux it's a normal pip install; on Windows you need a community wheel.
fixes: Linux/WSL: `pip install triton`.
Windows: install a community wheel — `pip install triton-windows` (woct0rdho/triton-windows) matching your Python+CUDA.
If the node just calls `torch.compile`, you can disable it (set `mode=eager` or wrap with `try/except`) — Triton becomes optional.

patterns.headline: xformers was built for a different PyTorch version.
patterns.cause: xformers C++ extensions are pinned to a specific torch ABI. Upgrading torch (or installing xformers built for the wrong torch) breaks the import.
fixes: Match versions: `pip install --upgrade --force-reinstall xformers` (will pull the matching torch).
Or, if you must keep your torch version, look up the matching xformers wheel at <https://github.com/facebookresearch/xformers/releases>.
Workaround: drop `--use-xformers` from launch args and use `--use-pytorch-cross-attention` (sdpa).

patterns.headline: `torch.compile` (Inductor) failed to compile the graph.
patterns.cause: torch.compile / Inductor graph compilation failed — often because of an unsupported op (custom CUDA kernel, certain control-flow patterns), a Triton mismatch, or a Windows-specific compiler-driver gap.
fixes: Easiest: disable the compile node (set `mode=eager`) — you keep correctness, lose 10-30 % speed.
If the error mentions `triton`, fix Triton first (see `triton_windows_missing`).
Try `mode=reduce-overhead` instead of `max-autotune`.
If you must compile: set `dynamic=True`, set `fullgraph=False`, and report the op in question to PyTorch.

patterns.headline: `diffusers` is too old for this model family.
patterns.cause: Newer model families (Flux, Wan, Qwen-Image, SD3.5) require recent `diffusers` releases. An older install raises `cannot import name`.
fixes: Upgrade: `pip install -U diffusers`. Minimums: Flux >=0.31, SD3.5 >=0.31, Wan >=0.32, Qwen-Image >=0.33.
Pin to a known-good version if you use a fork: `pip install diffusers==0.33.1`.
Restart ComfyUI after upgrade so the new module is loaded.

patterns.headline: This Hugging Face repo is gated — you need an access token.
patterns.cause: Some model weights (Flux-dev, SD3.5, certain Wan checkpoints) are gated. Downloading them requires accepting the licence on the HF page AND logging in with a token.
fixes: Visit the model's HF page in a browser, click 'Agree and access repository'.
Generate a token at <https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens> (read scope is enough).
Run: `huggingface-cli login` and paste the token. Or set `HF_TOKEN` env var.
Restart ComfyUI so the new credentials are picked up.

patterns.headline: Safetensors file is missing its `__metadata__` header.
patterns.cause: Some loaders (Kijai's wrapper, LoRA mergers) read `__metadata__` to know dtype / architecture / training config. An older or hand-built safetensors may omit it.
fixes: Re-download from the original source — most modern checkpoints include metadata.
If you built the file yourself: `save_file(state_dict, path, metadata={'format': 'pt'})`.
As a workaround, some loaders accept an explicit `dtype=` arg to skip metadata lookup.

patterns.cause: A widget value is outside its allowed range or has the wrong format.
fixes: Re-read the widget min/max in the node's tooltip.
If it's a string field, check for trailing whitespace or wrong separator.
